title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Classic Chicken Cacciatore Recipe | Pollo alla Cacciatora
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with our easy recipe. Tender chicken, rich tomato sauce, peppers & onions. Perfect Italian dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Hunter Style Chicken
Tips for the Best Chicken Cacciatore
seo.imageAlt: A rustic pot of authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with tender chicken, bell peppers, and tomatoes.
seo.imageCaption: Our classic Chicken Cacciatore, simmering to perfection – a true taste of Italy!

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora is a rustic Italian classic, featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory tomato sauce with bell peppers, onions, and aromatic herbs. This 'hunter-style' dish offers a comforting and flavorful experience, perfect for a hearty family meal. Its robust flavors transport you straight to the Italian countryside.

ingredients.notes: such as Pinot Grigio or Sauvignon Blanc

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken in batches, skin-side down first, until golden brown on all sides. Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion and sliced bell peppers to the same pot, adding a little more olive oil if needed. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables start to soften. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Add Wine
instructions.text: Pour the white wine into the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom with a wooden spoon. Let the wine simmer and reduce by about half, which should take 2-3 minutes. This adds a wonderful depth of flavor to the sauce.

instructions.title: Combine with Tomatoes and Herbs
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, fresh rosemary sprig, and chopped thyme. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.

instructions.title: Braise the Chicken
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is partially submerged. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the rosemary sprig. Let the Pollo alla Cacciatora rest for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Serve hot with your favorite side dishes like polenta, pasta, or crusty bread.

tips: For richer flavor, use bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces. The bones add depth and the skin helps brown the chicken beautifully before braising.
Don't skip the deglazing step with white wine. It's crucial for incorporating all the delicious browned bits (fond) from the bottom of the pot into your sauce.
Pollo alla Cacciatora often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article:
Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter's Style Chicken, is a timeless Italian classic. This hearty, flavorful dish brings the rustic charm of the Italian countryside to your dinner table, featuring tender chicken braised in a rich sauce of tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, garlic, herbs, and often wine. Creating an authentic Chicken Cacciatore is a deeply rewarding experience, resulting in a symphony of savory notes that is both comforting and satisfying. This comprehensive guide will walk you through preparing this exquisite dish, celebrating the robust flavors of Italy with every mouthwatering bite of this beloved Chicken Cacciatore.

## What is Chicken Cacciatore?
The name "Cacciatora" translates to "hunter's style," hinting at the dish's humble origins. Traditionally, Italian hunters prepared it using game birds, wild mushrooms, and local vegetables. While modern versions typically feature chicken, the essence remains: a rustic, one-pot wonder making the most of simple, fresh ingredients. At its core, Pollo alla Cacciatora slow-cooks chicken until fork-tender, enveloped in a vibrant, aromatic sauce. Regional variations exist – some favoring red wine, others white; some emphasizing tomatoes, others more on peppers and mushrooms – but chicken, vegetables, and a flavorful liquid base are constant. The beauty of Chicken Cacciatore lies in its simplicity and the depth of flavor achieved through patient simmering, allowing all ingredients to meld into a harmonious whole.

## Ingredients for Pollo alla Cacciatora
Crafting an exceptional Pollo alla Cacciatora begins with selecting the right ingredients:

*   **Chicken
